text:
Once upon a time, there was a wise old owl who lived in the heart of a dense forest. Every day, he would stretch his wings and gaze at the glorious sunset over the lake. One autumn day, while he was out on his usual patrol, he noticed something strange.

A small rabbit, terrified of owls, huddled in a clump of bushes. The owl crouched down quietly. After a moment, the rabbit burst from its hiding place, trembling. "Oh dear!" the owl chuckled. "Would you like some assistance?"

The rabbit scampered back across the clearing. The owl perched high. "How can I help you?" he asked kindly.

"Please, just go away," whimpered the rabbit.

"How about a cozy bath?" The owl pecked whimsically at a puddle. "You?"

"No!"

"Why not?" asked the owl.

"Why?" protested the rabbit.

"Well," the owl began with a wise tilting of his head.
